title: podman exec: exit status is always 0
body:
(at least for all cases I've tested):

    # podman run -d --name foo registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rhel:latest sleep 5m

    # podman exec foo /bin/false
    # echo $?
    0

    # podman exec foo /bin/bash -c 'exit 32'
    # echo $?
    0

docker propagates the exit status. However, neither docker-exec(1) nor podman-exec(1) actually specify a behavior.

comments.body: This should be fixed in #394 whenever I finally get that working - we were ignoring all error returns from the exec'd process.
Thank you! Sorry to have missed that in my search.
It's fine, I never opened an issue when I noticed the problem, and the PR is mainly fixing other things.
Partial fix landed in #412 - we now exit with an error code, but the code is not the one from the exec'd program.
@mheon Is this an issue with runc or something else?
@rhatdan Naw, this is just our code - I think the `podman` side of things isn't setting the right error code
